Package microui.core

Class BufferedView

java.lang.Object
microui.core.BufferedView
All Implemented Interfaces:
Visible
public abstract class BufferedView extends Object implements Visible
Abstract base class for views that render to an off-screen graphics buffer. BufferedView provides a foundation for components that need to render to a PGraphics buffer rather than directly to the main drawing surface.

This class implements the Visible interface to control rendering visibility and provides a template method pattern for subclasses to implement their specific rendering logic.

See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • BufferedView

      public BufferedView()

  • Method Details

    • draw

      public void draw(processing.core.PGraphics pGraphics)
      Renders the view to the specified graphics buffer if visible. This method checks visibility and delegates to the abstract render method.
      Parameters:
      pGraphics - the graphics buffer to render to
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if pGraphics is null

    • isVisible

      public boolean isVisible()
      Checks if the view is currently visible and should be rendered.
      Specified by:
      isVisible in interface Visible
      Returns:
      true if the view is visible, false otherwise

    • setVisible

      public void setVisible(boolean visible)
      Sets the visibility of the view. When invisible, the view will not be rendered even if draw() is called.
      Specified by:
      setVisible in interface Visible
      Parameters:
      visible - true to make the view visible, false to hide it
